lede: A look at October 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Coluche and Troian Bellisario, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Barack Obama
events.desc: US President Barack Obama signs the Matthew Shepard and James Byrd Jr. Hate Crimes Prevention Act.

events.title: Prospero (spacecraft)
events.desc: Prospero becomes the only British satellite to be launched by a British rocket.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Greece rejects Italy's ultimatum. Italy invades Greece through Albania a few hours later.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: A new Polish government in western Galicia is established, triggering the Polish–Ukrainian War.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: British troops attack and capture Chatterton Hill from the Continental Army.

events.title: 1707 Hōei earthquake
events.desc: The 1707 Hōei earthquake causes more than 5,000 deaths in Japan.

events.title: Treaty of Ripon
events.desc: The Treaty of Ripon is signed, ending the hostilities of the Second Bishops' War.

events.title: Ferdinand Magellan
events.desc: Ferdinand Magellan reaches the Pacific Ocean.

events.title: Ladislaus the Posthumous
events.desc: Ladislaus the Posthumous is crowned king of Bohemia in Prague.

events.title: Beijing
events.desc: Beijing is officially designated the capital of the Ming dynasty when the Forbidden City is completed.

births.name: Bill Gates
births.line: American businessman and philanthropist, co-founded Microsoft

births.name: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad
births.line: Iranian engineer and politician, 6th President of Iran

deaths.text: Tadeusz Mazowiecki, Polish journalist and politician, Prime Minister of Poland
Bolesław III Wrymouth, Duke of Poland from 1107 to 1138

observances.text: Christian feast day: Abgar V of Edessa (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Fidelis of Como (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Job of Pochayiv (repose) (Eastern Orthodox Church)
